person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henry_II_of_England
Father	Geoffrey V, Count of Anjou
Mother	Empress Matilda
Born	5 March 1133 Le Mans, Maine, Kingdom of France
Died	6 July 1189 (aged 56) Chinon Castle, Chinon, Touraine, Kingdom of France
Burial	Fontevraud Abbey, Anjou, France
Spouse	Eleanor of Aquitaine ​(m. 1152)​

Issue	
- William IX, Count of Poitiers
- Henry the Young King
- Matilda, Duchess of Saxony
- Richard I, King of England
- Geoffrey II, Duke of Brittany
- Eleanor, Queen of Castile
- Joan, Queen of Sicily
- John, King of England
Illegitimate:
- Geoffrey, Archbishop of York
- William, Earl of Salisbury

Henry II (5 March 1133 – 6 July 1189), also known as Henry Curtmantle (French: Court-manteau), Henry FitzEmpress or Henry Plantagenet, was King of England from 1154 until his death in 1189. He was the first king of the House of Plantagenet. King Louis VII of France made him Duke of Normandy in 1150. Henry became Count of Anjou and Maine upon the death of his father, Count Geoffrey V, in 1151. His marriage in 1152 to Eleanor of Aquitaine, whose marriage to Louis VII had recently been annulled, made him Duke of Aquitaine. He became Count of Nantes by treaty in 1185. Before he was 40 he controlled England, large parts of Wales, the eastern half of Ireland and the western half of France; an area that was later called the Angevin Empire. At various times, Henry also partially controlled Scotland and the Duchy of Brittany.

Henry became actively involved by the age of 14 in the efforts of his mother Matilda, daughter of Henry I of England, to claim the throne of England, then occupied by Stephen of Blois. Stephen agreed to a peace treaty after Henry's military expedition to England in 1153, and Henry inherited the kingdom on Stephen's death a year later. Henry was an energetic and ruthless ruler, driven by a desire to restore the lands and privileges of his grandfather Henry I. During the early years of his reign the younger Henry restored the royal administration in England, re-established hegemony over Wales and gained full control over his lands in Anjou, Maine and Touraine. Henry's desire to reform the relationship with the Church led to conflict with his former friend Thomas Becket, the Archbishop of Canterbury. This controversy lasted for much of the 1160s and resulted in Becket's murder in 1170. Henry soon came into conflict with Louis VII, and the two rulers fought what has been termed a "cold war" over several decades. Henry expanded his empire at Louis's expense, taking Brittany and pushing east into central France and south into Toulouse; despite numerous peace conferences and treaties, no lasting agreement was reached.

Henry and Eleanor had eight children—three daughters and five sons. Three of his sons would be king, though Henry the Young King was named his father's co-ruler rather than a stand-alone king. As the sons grew up, tensions over the future inheritance of the empire began to emerge, encouraged by Louis and his son King Philip II. In 1173 Henry's heir apparent, "Young Henry", rebelled in protest; he was joined by his brothers Richard (later king) and Geoffrey and by their mother, Eleanor. France, Scotland, Brittany, Flanders, and Boulogne allied themselves with the rebels. The Great Revolt was only defeated by Henry's vigorous military action and talented local commanders, many of them "new men" appointed for their loyalty and administrative skills. Young Henry and Geoffrey revolted again in 1183, resulting in Young Henry's death. The Norman invasion of Ireland provided lands for his youngest son John (later king), but Henry struggled to find ways to satisfy all his sons' desires for land and immediate power. By 1189, Young Henry and Geoffrey were dead, and Philip successfully played on Richard's fears that Henry II would make John king, leading to a final rebellion. Decisively defeated by Philip and Richard and suffering from a bleeding ulcer, Henry retreated to Chinon Castle in Anjou. He died soon afterwards and was succeeded by Richard.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henry_II_of_England

zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry II King of England於1133年3月5日出生在法國薩特勒 曼斯時，他的父親Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjou19歲，母親Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress31歲。他於1152年5月18日與Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre在法國努維勒-阿吉泰吉洛德波迪奧結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1189年7月6日在法國崔英德-吉洛易農享年56歲，埋葬在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易。

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry II King of England was born on 5 March 1133, in Le Mans, Sarthe, France, his father, Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjou, was 19 and his mother, Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress, was 31. He married Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre on 18 May 1152, in Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 6 July 1189, in Chinon, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, France, at the age of 56, and was buried in Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry II King of England est né le 5 mars 1133, à Le Mans, Sarthe, France, son père, Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjou, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre le 18 mai 1152, à Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 6 juillet 1189, à Chinon, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, France, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry II King of England am 5. März 1133 in Le Mans, Sarthe, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjou 19 und seine Mutter Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress 31. Er hat am 18. Mai 1152 in Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 6. Juli 1189 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Chinon, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, Frankreich und wurde in Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry II King of England è nato il il 5 marzo 1133 nel Le Mans, Sarthe, Francia, il suo padre, Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjou, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre il 18 maggio 1152, nel Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 6 luglio 1189, nel Chinon, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, Francia, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry II King of Englandが1133年3月5日にフランスサルスル マンスで生まれた時，父Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjouは19歳，母Matilda of England Holy Roman Empressは31歳であった。1152年5月18日にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインギロンドボルディーウックスでAliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1189年7月6日にフランスセントアインドレ-エト-ロイアーチノンで56歳で死去し，フランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーメインエ-エト-ロイアーにて埋葬された。

ko.Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loir: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 cha테오-드-로이르
ko.Fontrevault Abbey, Maine et Loire, Franc: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르

ko.lifeSummary: Henry II King of England(이)가 1133년 3월 5일 프랑스 사르테 일 먼스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjou(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1152년 5월 18일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 지론드 볼도스에서 Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1189년 7월 6일 프랑스 세트르 인드레-트-로이르 친넌에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry II King of England nasceu em 5 de março de 1133, em Le Mans, Sarthe, França, seu pai, Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjou, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre em 18 de maio de 1152, em Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 6 de julho de 1189, em Chinon, Indre-et-Loire, Centre, França, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França.

ru.Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire: ангирс, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry II King of England родился 5 марта 1133 г. в местности л манс, сарт, Франция, его отцу по имени Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjou было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre 18 мая 1152 г. в местности бордокс, гиронд, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 6 июля 1189 г. в местности чинон, индре-ет-лоир, кентр, Франция в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry II King of England nació el 5 de marzo de 1133, en Le Mans, Sarthe, Francia, su papá, Geoffrey Plantagenet Count of Anjou, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre el 18 de mayo de 1152, en Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas.
